counters.description: ashe is annoying to play against, as it's difficult to get on her. when you walk up she slows you for ages and running away is near impossible aswell.
brand deals a lot of damage. because of his range and possibility to stun a difficult matchup. on the other hand, getting on top op him likely results in a kill.

janna's tornado will interrupt every single engage you try to do. when teamfighting she will ult to create space between you and her team. this matchup is very difficult, but winnable if you manage to surprise her with a flash engage.

karma will definitly poke you out of lane, so be careful for that. when you manage to stay healthy in lane, you can try to engage. karma will be able to escape using her w or e, so a hard matchup overall.
lulu will only do one thing in this lane and that is polymorph either you or your adc when you engage. try to find a pick when she isn't in lane or shortly disengage when the polymorph lands.

even though milio is a very short ranged enchanter, he will still outrange you and be hard to catch out because of his w with extra attack range or his e that gives movement speed. an experienced milio will be able to disrupt your engage using his q and when you finally manage to lock someone down, his r has a cleansing effect which saves enemies that you hit with your e. a pretty annoying matchup!
morgana is a pain for every engage support player. her q will give you afk warnings and her spell shield will deny your combo. don't play this matchup for your own good.

nami  is another enchanter with some disengage built into her kit to make your life difficult. her e is slowing you and her q stuns you for a second or so. her ult is the perfect tool to stop you from engaging on her team so definitely try to play around this.
poppy's w will stop you from engaging as it blocks all dashes including your w. play around this or you will insta-lose your lane, because you can't play the game.
renata glasc is an annoying opponent as her poke slows you and a well-timed q can cancel or displace your combo. play around her cooldowns early as they are pretty high.

shaco has boxes which fear you and that makes it really annoying to play against him. when looking for bush control or engages you will definetely step into one, so an early oracle lens can help a lot.

soraka is a difficult matchup as she is good at sustaining and her q will slow you, which makes your vulnerable. when engaging she will place her e under her carry so you don't get to finish your combo. play around this cooldown! highly adviced to buy anti-heal.

swain is a "support" with a lot of damage, crowd control en healing. when playing against him, getting anti-healing is a near must for either you or your adc. for teamfights: if swain ults you can knock him away to create distance and let his ult run out. after that you can freely re-engage.
vel'koz is another of those "supports" that does a lot of damage. just try to survive lane by staying healthy and punish him for his cooldowns.

xerath is also a "support" with damage and crowd control. stay healthy in lane and play around his cooldowns, especially his e which is pretty long. when you get on top of him, you can burst him down quickly with the help of your adc.

zilean is annoying for the fact that he has a 99% on click slow in the later stages of the game and a revive with a low cooldown. when you find a good engage, don't be tunnel visioned onto them as they will likely get ulted.
zyra her plants can be annoying and she has some steady poke with her q. play around her e and punish her when she misplaces her plants or e.

synergies.description: aphelios is a strong hypercarry without mobility. you can help him by creating distance between him and the enemies.

ashe is another carry without mobility. her slow however can prove useful, because when you cc the enemy they have a harder time to get away because they are slowed. this will make you hit your e easier.
caitlyn has a lot of range and you don't. can cause a miss match in positioning because the distance between you and her is very big.
works perfect for an aggressive playstyle. draven has a lot of damage to follow up on your engage which will lead to a lot of kills.
ezreal has a lot of burst damage dependant on his build. if he does burst a lot, you can easily lock down a target and burst him down. he is not a hypercarry so think about itemizing correctly.
jhin is pretty awkward to play around. he has a lot of movement speed to kite for himself, but his burst is not high enough to finish someone within a combo.
strong hypercarry that requires some peeling early on in a teamfight, but when her passive is active look for aggressive picks as she can easily follow up.
the all in potential with kai'sa is pretty huge as she can easily follow up with her r and burst someone down with her passive. great combinations and fun to play with.

while you cc your enemies, kalista gets the time to dance around them and hit them with a spear or 2. when you are in trouble or not able to find an angle, she can use her ult and you can throw yourself closer to the enemies. great pairing.
just don't play alistar with him. he needs a lot of good peeling and buffing and as alistar this might feel awkward in game.
great all in potential to burst enemies down quickly. look to play aggressive and get your lucian ahead as he is really strong in the early and mid game.
a lot of damage and good wombo combo synergy. cc targets in het ult and you are guaranteed getting some kills. strong teamfighting duo, might be hard to control the lane.

good all in potential and also close range which makes you two play close together. the extra xp sharing will compensate for the missing xp from roaming and her extra healing passive will scale well with your passive.
great all in combo. your engage enables samira to proc her passive and dash in. your cc can keep enemies in place long enough for samira to evaporate them. fun to play combo!
senna is a champion you don't see that often in adc anymore. when you do, her range is quite long so a lot of distance between you 2. she can however help you sustain through lane with her q.
sivir needs some time to scale and do damage, so laning is all about surviving. as alistar you want to engage, so this is a typical combo where you want to roam a lot while sivir is safely farming.

smolder benefits from strong peeling support including alistar. laning will be really hard with this duo, but if you manage to survive and go late you can win every game. just make sure your little dragon doesn't die and you're good to go!
great all in potential from level 2 onwards. tristana can easily follow you with her jump and she has the burst damage to burst down enemies. great combo for an aggressive botlane.
strong teamfighting duo. while you engage twitch can look for a good angle to start firing away at the enemy. both aren't the strongest laners so survive lane and win game.

if he goes for the poke build, he is dinstanced a lot from you and he lacks some burst damage early. when building on hit he needs decent peel as he doesn't have any mobility built in. leads to awkward moments in lane.
strong hypercarry which needs the right peel. because of the short range of vayne you can play closely together and it's much easier for the adc to follow up with some damage.
your cc can help xayah setup her feathers and these can burst down targets easily. nice aggressive combo, but becareful because xayah is little to no mobility besides her ult.
fun all-in combo where you knockup perfectly sets up for a yasuo ult. because of yasuo's dash he can easily follow you when engaging aswell.
zeri is a hypercarry with built in mobility which makes your life easier. her damage early is fairly low, so you need to survive the laning phase.
aphelios is a strong hypercarry without mobility. you can help him by creating distance between him and the enemies.

ashe is another carry without mobility. her slow however can prove useful, because when you cc the enemy they have a harder time to get away because they are slowed. this will make you hit your e easier.
